name: Afimoxifene
description: Afimoxifene (4-Hydroxytamoxifen, trade name TamoGel) is a new estrogen inhibitor under investigation for a variety of estrogen-dependent conditions, including cyclic breast pain and gynecomastia. TamoGel is formulated using Enhanced Hydroalcoholic Gel (EHG) Technology. This technology enables percutaneous delivery of drugs that cannot be delivered orally. It is being developed by Ascent Therapeutics.
indication: For the potential treatment of menstrual-cycle related mastalgia, fibrocystic breast disease, breast disease, gynecomastia and Keloid scarring.
mechanismOfAction: Afimoxifene binds to estrogen receptors (ER), inducing a conformational change in the receptor. This results in a blockage or change in the expression of estrogen dependent genes.

interactions.name: Ospemifene
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Afimoxifene is combined with Ospemifene.

interactions.name: Trastuzumab
interactions.description: Trastuzumab may increase the cardiotoxic activities of Afimoxifene.

interactions.name: Paclitaxel
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Paclitaxel is combined with Afimoxifene.

interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Docetaxel is combined with Afimoxifene.

interactions.name: Cabazitaxel
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Cabazitaxel is combined with Afimoxifene.
